title: Cyclobalanopsis paucidentata
taxonomy: Cyclobalanopsis paucidentata is a species of deciduous tree in the family Fagaceae. It belongs to the genus Cyclobalanopsis, which is a group of oak trees native to East Asia. Cyclobalanopsis paucidentata is further classified as a member of the subgenus Cyclobalanopsis and the section Cyclobalanopsis. The species is native to China and is part of the larger family of Fagaceae, which includes many other types of oaks and beeches. This classification is based on its characteristics and genetic relationships to other species within the family.
uses: Cyclobalanopsis paucidentata, also known as the Taiwan oak, has been traditionally used in various folk medicine practices. Its leaves and fruits are said to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may contribute to its medicinal uses. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is believed to have a cooling effect and is used to treat fever, cough, and other respiratory issues. Additionally, the plant's extracts are reported to exhibit antimicrobial and antiviral activities, which may be beneficial in preventing infections. Further research is needed to fully understand and validate the medicinal potential of Cyclobalanopsis paucidentata.

The leaves of the plant are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which may help to protect against cell damage and reduce inflammation.
